steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: Bryan opposed the Constitution, then published essays disparaging federalists who supported it. These aren't contradictory—the essays naturally express his opposition. The key signal is: this is a natural fit, not a contrast.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content:
The logical relationship is addition/fit — Bryan opposed the Constitution and then took an action that naturally expressed that opposition. The blank needs a transition signaling that what follows is an appropriate, fitting consequence.
  • "by comparison,": signals contrast between two things, so fails — no comparison is being made. ✗
  • "fittingly,": signals that what follows is an apt, natural expression of what preceded it, so fits perfectly. ✓
  • "nevertheless,": signals contrast/concession (despite X, Y still happened), so fails — there is no opposition here. ✗
  • "in other words,": signals restatement of the same idea, so fails — publishing essays is a new action, not a rephrasing. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
